data.title: Economist warns of potential bailout if Trump family-backed crypto ventures fail, reports $5 billion windfall, predicts bubble.
data.description: Economist Justin Wolfers expressed worries on Wednesday about a potential government bailout if the Trump family-associated cryptocurrency projects collapse. During an MSNBC interview, Wolfers highlighted concerns over the Trump family's alleged $5 billion gain from the recent World Liberty Financial (WLFI) token launch. He noted that most of the president's wealth currently originates from the cryptocurrency sector, which includes Official Trump (CRYPTO: TRUMP) and Official Melania (CRYPTO: MELANIA) coins. Wolfers cautioned that these ventures are being marketed to working- and middle-class Americans as investments when they are not, raising the specter of a financial bubble. He emphasized the need for clarity that during a possible crypto crash, there should be no expectation of a federal rescue. Wolfers called on Republican congressional representatives to declare their stance on this issue before potential conflicts of interest involving the President emerge.

data.title: Pan American Silver acquires MAG Silver, completing deal for all shares; transaction adds 44% interest in Juani.
data.description: Pan American Silver Corp. and MAG Silver Corp. have officially completed the acquisition deal, where Pan American acquired all of the MAG Silver Corp.'s issued and outstanding common shares. This acquisition was carried out through a plan of arrangement, adding a 44% joint venture interest for Pan American in the Juani project. Pan American Silver Corp. is listed on NYSE (PAAS) and TSX (PAAS), while MAG Silver Corp. trades on NYSE American (MAG) and TSX (MAG).

data.title: Ingevity sells North Charleston CTO refinery and Industrial Specialties product line to Mainstream Pine Products.
data.description: An agreement has been reached by Ingevity to sell the North Charleston CTO refinery along with the majority of the Industrial Specialties product line to Mainstream Pine Products. The deal involves a significant transaction in North Charleston, S.C. This strategic move will have a notable impact on the operations and product offerings of both companies involved.

data.title: Shoe Carnival beats expectations: Q2 2025 EPS at $0.70, 270 basis points profit margin increase to 38.8%. Positive sales growth noted.
data.description: Fort Mill, South Carolina-based Shoe Carnival, Inc. (Nasdaq: SCVL), a top retailer of footwear and accessories, recently released its second quarter results for the period ending on August 2, 2025. In this quarter, the company exceeded expectations by achieving earnings per share (EPS) of $0.70, surpassing the consensus by over 20 percent. They also expanded their gross profit margin by 270 basis points to reach 38.8 percent. Additionally, Shoe Carnival saw positive comparable sales and margin growth during August. Looking ahead, the company updated its Fiscal 2025 outlook with these strong performances in mind.

data.title: BTC ETFs experience highest inflows since early August due to strategic moves from Ethereum amidst economic uncertainty. Analysts advise caution.
data.description: The increase in BTC ETF inflows was driven by strategic repositioning and shift from Ethereum, as per analysts. This trend emerged amidst a backdrop of macroeconomic uncertainty.

data.title: Stardust Power implements reverse stock split to meet Nasdaq requirements for long-term growth.
data.description: Stardust Power recently revealed a reverse stock split strategy as part of their plan to meet compliance requirements with Nasdaq and ensure sustainable growth in the long term. This move is aimed at reinforcing their position in the market.

data.title: OM price stabilizes at $0.20 amid bearish signals, up 8% this week. $25M buyback news affects technical indicators.
data.description: The price of OM has maintained a crucial support level at $0.20 despite a weekly increase of 8%. Technical indicators are sending conflicting signals following the announcement of a $25 million buyback.
